metadata.title: Sub-Surface Clay Pot Olla Irrigation Blueprint

public_links.title: Wikipedia: Olla Irrigation Mechanics & Soil Moisture Physics
FAO: Small-Scale Irrigation Techniques for Arid Regions

technical_documents.description: An ultra-efficient, passive micro-irrigation blueprint that leverages the natural soil moisture tension and hydraulic filtration of underground unglazed terracotta vessels. By embedding highly porous clay pots directly within the crop root zones, this system eliminates evaporative overhead water losses entirely, slashing aggregate irrigation water usage by up to 70% while optimizing localized plant hydration cycles.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Smallholder farmers in semi-arid regions lose over 50–70% of vital irrigation water to rapid surface evaporation and deep soil percolation before plant root networks can effectively absorb it, causing severe nutrient leaching, depressed crop yields, and high operational water-pumping overheads during critical dry seasons.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The system utilizes unglazed terracotta vessels calibrated with an 8mm porous wall thickness, buried at a subsurface depth of 25cm directly within crop rows. Water distribution operates autonomously via soil-moisture tension gradients (suction forces): as the surrounding soil dries, hydrostatic capillary action draws water out through the clay vessel's micropores at a dynamic rate that perfectly matches root-zone transpiration demands. This self-regulating fluid loop achieves a 95% uniform application efficiency without requiring computerized timing valves, active pressure systems, or on-grid electricity.

bill_of_materials.name: High-Porosity Unglazed Terracotta Pots (30cm Height, x10)
Weatherproof Clay Sealing Lids or Flat Thermal Stones
Rigid PVC Water-Fill Tubes (50cm Length, 25mm Diameter)
Coarse Capillary Sand Matrix (for root-zone backfill)

specifications.irrigation_radius: 60cm effective soil moisture radius per pot

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Source unglazed terracotta pots; test porosity by filling with water — should bead on exterior within 30 min.
Step 2: Drill a 25mm hole in the pot base for the fill tube; seal the tube-basin joint with silicone.
Step 3: Dig holes 30cm deep at 60cm spacing between crop rows; bury pots with rims 5cm below surface.
Step 4: Backfill with coarse sand around pots for capillary continuity to surrounding soil.
Step 5: Cover with clay lid; fill via tube weekly. Monitor soil moisture 15cm from pot to calibrate interval.

comments.text: Reduced our tomato field water usage from 200L/day to 60L/day. Plants are healthier too.
